pairs.a: whats the capital of Australia
pairs.b: whats the capital of Austria
pairs.note: Two country names one letter apart will embed almost identically but have different answers.

pairs.a: How do I center a div horizontally in Tailwind CSS?
pairs.b: How do I center a div horizontally in Bootstrap?
pairs.note: Same layout task but the framework determines the actual class names to use.

pairs.a: What year did Einstein win the Nobel Prize in Physics?
pairs.b: What year did Niels Bohr win the Nobel Prize in Physics?
pairs.note: Both are early 20th century physicists in an identical template, but the years differ.

pairs.a: What is the best way to manage global state in a React app?
pairs.b: What is the best way to manage global state in a Vue app?
pairs.note: React and Vue have entirely different state ecosystems despite the identical phrasing.

pairs.a: How do I reverse a string in Python?
pairs.b: How do I reverse a string in Ruby?
pairs.note: Two scripting languages in the same slot produce different idiomatic code.

pairs.a: How do I set up a VPC peering connection in AWS?
pairs.b: How do I set up a VPC peering connection in Azure?
pairs.note: Cloud provider swap changes every console step and CLI command in the answer.

pairs.a: Do I need a visa to travel to Japan as a US citizen?
pairs.b: Do I need a visa to travel to Vietnam as a US citizen?
pairs.note: Destination country determines the visa answer even though the sentence is otherwise identical.

pairs.a: How long should I bake salmon fillets at 400F?
pairs.b: How long should I bake chicken breasts at 400F?
pairs.note: Same oven temperature and phrasing, but cook times for fish and poultry differ a lot.

pairs.a: Who wrote the novel Brave New World?
pairs.b: Who wrote the novel Nineteen Eighty-Four?
pairs.note: Two canonical dystopian novels sit in the same slot but have different authors.

pairs.a: What is the default port for PostgreSQL?
pairs.b: What is the default port for MySQL?
pairs.note: Database swap yields 5432 vs 3306 despite near-identical token overlap.

pairs.a: How do I install Docker on Ubuntu 22.04?
pairs.b: How do I install Podman on Ubuntu 22.04?
pairs.note: Container runtimes are frequently discussed together but have different install steps.

pairs.a: What is the expense ratio of VTI?
pairs.b: What is the expense ratio of VOO?
pairs.note: Two popular index ETFs with three-letter tickers and different fee figures.

pairs.a: How much protein is in 100g of chicken breast?
pairs.b: How much protein is in 100g of salmon?
pairs.note: Nutrition template stays fixed while the food changes the numeric answer.

pairs.a: Convert 100 USD to EUR
pairs.b: Convert 250 USD to EUR
pairs.note: Only the amount changes, and most embedding models barely register the digit swap.

pairs.a: What are the top 5 highest grossing movies of all time?
pairs.b: What are the top 10 highest grossing movies of all time?
pairs.note: A cached list of 5 is an incomplete answer for a request for 10.

pairs.a: What are the new language features in Java 17?
pairs.b: What are the new language features in Java 21?
pairs.note: Both are LTS releases but the feature sets are completely different.

pairs.a: How long do I roast a 3 lb pork shoulder at 325F?
pairs.b: How long do I roast a 6 lb pork shoulder at 325F?
pairs.note: Weight roughly doubles the cook time while the sentence is otherwise identical.

pairs.a: What is a 15 percent tip on an 80 dollar bill?
pairs.b: What is a 20 percent tip on an 80 dollar bill?
pairs.note: Percentage swap changes the arithmetic result but almost nothing in the wording.

pairs.a: Give me a 7 day itinerary for Lisbon
pairs.b: Give me a 10 day itinerary for Lisbon
pairs.note: Trip length changes the whole plan structure, not just its length.

pairs.a: How much do I need to save monthly to reach 100k in 10 years?
pairs.b: How much do I need to save monthly to reach 100k in 15 years?
pairs.note: The time horizon drives the monthly figure, so serving the cached answer misleads.

pairs.a: What breaks when upgrading from Python 3.9 to 3.11?
pairs.b: What breaks when upgrading from Python 3.9 to 3.12?
pairs.note: One digit in the target version changes the list of removals and deprecations.

pairs.a: Monthly payment on a 300000 mortgage at 6 percent over 30 years?
pairs.b: Monthly payment on a 300000 mortgage at 6 percent over 15 years?
pairs.note: Term length materially changes the payment while sharing every other token.

pairs.a: How many calories are in 2 slices of pepperoni pizza?
pairs.b: How many calories are in 4 slices of pepperoni pizza?
pairs.note: Quantity swap doubles the answer and is trivially easy for a cache to miss.

pairs.a: Write a regex that matches exactly 3 digits
pairs.b: Write a regex that matches exactly 4 digits
pairs.note: The cached pattern would be silently wrong for the new count.

pairs.a: What is the resting heart rate range for a 30 year old man?
pairs.b: What is the resting heart rate range for a 60 year old man?
pairs.note: Age changes the reference range in an otherwise word-for-word identical prompt.

pairs.a: How do I migrate my app to React 18?
pairs.b: How do I migrate my app to React 19?
pairs.note: Adjacent major versions have distinct migration steps and breaking changes.

pairs.a: Convert 50 km to miles
pairs.b: Convert 50 km to meters
pairs.note: Same source quantity, different target unit, so the numeric result is unrelated.

pairs.a: Convert 100 USD to EUR
pairs.b: Convert 100 USD to GBP
pairs.note: Currency codes are three-letter tokens that embedding models treat as near synonyms.

pairs.a: What is 180 C in Fahrenheit?
pairs.b: What is 180 C in Kelvin?
pairs.note: Temperature scale swap changes the answer from 356 to 453 with a one word edit.

pairs.a: Convert 2 cups of flour to grams
pairs.b: Convert 2 cups of flour to ounces
pairs.note: Baking conversions differ by target unit while the ingredient and amount stay fixed.

pairs.a: What time is 3pm EST in UTC?
pairs.b: What time is 3pm EST in PST?
pairs.note: Timezone abbreviations are near-identical tokens with a several hour difference.

pairs.a: How many megabytes are in 4 GB?
pairs.b: How many kilobytes are in 4 GB?
pairs.note: Data unit swap moves the answer by three orders of magnitude.

pairs.a: Convert 10 Mbps to MB/s
pairs.b: Convert 10 Mbps to Gbps
pairs.note: Bits versus bytes versus scale prefix, all inside a very short similar string.

pairs.a: How much is 90 kg in pounds?
pairs.b: How much is 90 kg in stone?
pairs.note: Both are imperial weight units so the prompts are extremely close in embedding space.

pairs.a: How do I enable two factor authentication on GitHub?
pairs.b: How do I disable two factor authentication on GitHub?
pairs.note: Enable and disable are near synonyms to an embedding model but opposite instructions.

pairs.a: How do I turn on format on save in VS Code?
pairs.b: How do I turn off format on save in VS Code?
pairs.note: Single word polarity flip inverts the setting value the answer should give.

pairs.a: Which foods should I eat before a long run?
pairs.b: Which foods should I not eat before a long run?
pairs.note: Adding not produces a disjoint list, but adds almost no embedding distance.

pairs.a: Give me reasons why you should use microservices
pairs.b: Give me reasons why you should not use microservices
pairs.note: The negated form asks for the opposite argument entirely.

pairs.a: How do I make a column nullable in Postgres?
pairs.b: How do I make a column not nullable in Postgres?
pairs.note: DROP NOT NULL versus SET NOT NULL, separated by a single token.

pairs.a: Which countries require a visa for US citizens?
pairs.b: Which countries do not require a visa for US citizens?
pairs.note: Complementary sets of countries hidden behind a two word negation.

pairs.a: Why does my Docker container keep restarting?
pairs.b: Why does my Docker container not restart?
pairs.note: Crash looping and failing to restart are opposite failure modes with opposite fixes.

pairs.a: How do I increase the JVM heap size?
pairs.b: How do I decrease the JVM heap size?
pairs.note: Increase and decrease sit close in embedding space but flip the intended flag value.

pairs.a: What are the advantages of using a monorepo?
pairs.b: What are the disadvantages of using a monorepo?
pairs.note: Pros and cons of the same topic are semantically near but factually opposite.

pairs.a: What foods raise your blood pressure?
pairs.b: What foods lower your blood pressure?
pairs.note: Raise and lower are antonyms that co-occur constantly in health text.

pairs.a: How do I gain weight on a vegetarian diet?
pairs.b: How do I lose weight on a vegetarian diet?
pairs.note: Opposite goals with the same dietary constraint and identical sentence frame.

pairs.a: How do I make my sourdough more sour?
pairs.b: How do I make my sourdough less sour?
pairs.note: More and less differ by two characters but reverse the fermentation advice.

pairs.a: How do I scale up a Kubernetes deployment?
pairs.b: How do I scale down a Kubernetes deployment?
pairs.note: Up and down are strongly co-located in embeddings yet mean opposite operations.

pairs.a: Why is my house too humid in the winter?
pairs.b: Why is my house too dry in the winter?
pairs.note: Humid versus dry inverts the diagnosis and the recommended appliance.

pairs.a: Explain how OAuth 2.0 works
pairs.b: Explain how OAuth 2.0 works to a 5 year old
pairs.note: The audience constraint demands a totally different register and level of detail.

pairs.a: Summarize the causes of World War 1 in one sentence
pairs.b: Write a 2000 word essay on the causes of World War 1
pairs.note: Same subject matter but the output length requirement differs by three orders of magnitude.

pairs.a: Give me a code example of a Python decorator
pairs.b: Explain the theory behind Python decorators
pairs.note: Code versus conceptual explanation are different deliverables for the same topic.

pairs.a: What is a Kalman filter?
pairs.b: Derive the Kalman filter equations step by step
pairs.note: A definition cannot substitute for a full mathematical derivation.

pairs.a: Give me a one line description of Kubernetes
pairs.b: Give me a detailed architecture overview of Kubernetes
pairs.note: Depth demand differs sharply while topic tokens dominate the embedding.

pairs.a: How does HTTPS work?
pairs.b: How does HTTPS work at the packet level?
pairs.note: Four extra words move the answer from an overview to a byte-by-byte handshake trace.

pairs.a: Write a haiku about the ocean
pairs.b: Write a sonnet about the ocean
pairs.note: Identical subject with a strict but different structural format requirement.

pairs.a: Give me bullet points on the French Revolution
pairs.b: Give me a detailed year by year timeline of the French Revolution
pairs.note: Output format and granularity both change even though the topic is unchanged.

pairs.a: What is the weather in Chicago today?
pairs.b: What is the weather in Chicago tomorrow?
pairs.note: One word shifts the forecast window and invalidates any cached response.

pairs.a: Who is the current CEO of Starbucks?
pairs.b: Who was the CEO of Starbucks in 2010?
pairs.note: Present tense versus a historical year point to different people.

pairs.a: What are the best JavaScript frameworks in 2024?
pairs.b: What are the best JavaScript frameworks in 2025?
pairs.note: Year tokens are nearly interchangeable in embedding space but scope the answer.

pairs.a: What was the S&P 500 closing price today?
pairs.b: What was the S&P 500 closing price yesterday?
pairs.note: Market data is date-specific and a stale cached figure is simply wrong.

pairs.a: What is the IRS standard deduction for tax year 2023?
pairs.b: What is the IRS standard deduction for tax year 2024?
pairs.note: Tax figures are indexed yearly so the cached amount would be out of date.

pairs.a: When is the next SpaceX launch?
pairs.b: When was the last SpaceX launch?
pairs.note: Next versus last flips the direction in time with almost no lexical change.

pairs.a: What are current 30 year mortgage rates?
pairs.b: What were 30 year mortgage rates last year?
pairs.note: Present versus past rates share nearly every token but not the number.

pairs.a: Is Postgres better than MySQL for analytics workloads?
pairs.b: Is MySQL better than Postgres for analytics workloads?
pairs.note: Identical token bag with the comparison reversed, so the framing of the answer flips.

pairs.a: Convert 500 EUR to USD
pairs.b: Convert 500 USD to EUR
pairs.note: Bag-of-words identical, but the conversion direction changes the result entirely.

pairs.a: How do I migrate a project from Redux to Zustand?
pairs.b: How do I migrate a project from Zustand to Redux?
pairs.note: Migration direction determines which library the code examples target.

pairs.a: How do I convert a JSON string to a Python dict?
pairs.b: How do I convert a Python dict to a JSON string?
pairs.note: json.loads versus json.dumps, distinguished only by argument order in the sentence.

pairs.a: Translate this paragraph from Spanish to English
pairs.b: Translate this paragraph from English to Spanish
pairs.note: Source and target language swap produces output in a different language.

pairs.a: How much faster is Rust than Go at JSON parsing?
pairs.b: How much faster is Go than Rust at JSON parsing?
pairs.note: Reversing the subject of the comparison inverts the premise being tested.

pairs.a: Should I switch from iPhone to Android?
pairs.b: Should I switch from Android to iPhone?
pairs.note: Same two platforms, opposite starting point, so the tradeoffs discussed differ.

pairs.a: What is the formula to convert Celsius to Fahrenheit?
pairs.b: What is the formula to convert Fahrenheit to Celsius?
pairs.note: Inverse formulas that share every word except the order of two unit names.

pairs.a: How do I undo my last git commit?
pairs.b: I committed by mistake in git, how do I take that commit back?
pairs.note: Different wording and framing but the same underlying git operation is wanted.

pairs.a: How do I center a div horizontally and vertically with CSS?
pairs.b: What is the best way to perfectly center a div using CSS?
pairs.note: Perfectly centering is the common idiom for both axes, so the need is identical.

pairs.a: What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
pairs.b: How do TCP and UDP differ from one another?
pairs.note: Noun form versus verb form of the same comparison question.

pairs.a: Why is the sky blue?
pairs.b: What makes the sky appear blue?
pairs.note: Classic explanation request reworded with no change in the information need.

pairs.a: What causes jet lag?
pairs.b: Why do people get jet lag?
pairs.note: Cause and reason phrasings of one physiological question.

pairs.a: How do I check which process is using port 3000?
pairs.b: Something is holding port 3000, how do I find out what it is?
pairs.note: Conversational framing of the exact same diagnostic command request.

pairs.a: What does the SOLID acronym stand for in OOP?
pairs.b: Can you list what each letter of SOLID means in object oriented design?
pairs.note: Expanding an acronym is the same task whether asked as stands for or list letters.

pairs.a: How long should I boil eggs for hard boiled yolks?
pairs.b: What is the boiling time to get hard boiled eggs?
pairs.note: Both ask for the same cooking duration with reworded surface form.

pairs.a: What is the difference between == and === in JavaScript?
pairs.b: Explain how == differs from === in JavaScript
pairs.note: Question versus imperative phrasing of one well defined language semantics question.

pairs.a: How do I flatten a nested list in Python?
pairs.b: How do I turn a list of lists into one flat list in Python?
pairs.note: Flatten and turn into one flat list describe the identical operation.

pairs.a: What is a closure in JavaScript?
pairs.b: Can you explain what JavaScript closures are?
pairs.note: Singular definition request restated as a plural explanation request.

pairs.a: How do I renew my US passport?
pairs.b: What is the process for renewing a US passport?
pairs.note: Procedural question asked two ways with identical scope and answer.

pairs.a: How much water should an adult drink each day?
pairs.b: What is the recommended daily water intake for adults?
pairs.note: Colloquial and clinical phrasings of the same daily intake question.

pairs.a: How do I kill a process running on a port on macOS?
pairs.b: Hi, could you please tell me how to kill a process on a port on macOS? Thanks!
pairs.note: Greeting and thanks wrapper adds tokens but zero change to the information need.

pairs.a: Explain the difference between HTTP and HTTPS
pairs.b: Hello! Would you mind explaining the difference between HTTP and HTTPS? Thank you.
pairs.note: Formal hedging around an otherwise identical explanation request.

pairs.a: Give me a recipe for banana bread
pairs.b: Hey, could you please give me a recipe for banana bread?
pairs.note: Casual greeting plus please, with the same recipe request underneath.

pairs.a: What is the capital of Norway?
pairs.b: Good morning, I was wondering what the capital of Norway is?
pairs.note: Indirect polite framing of a single fact lookup.

pairs.a: How do I set up SSH keys for GitHub?
pairs.b: Could you kindly walk me through setting up SSH keys for GitHub? Thanks a lot.
pairs.note: Extra courtesy language does not change the setup steps required.

pairs.a: Convert 72 F to Celsius
pairs.b: Please convert 72 F to Celsius, thank you.
pairs.note: Politeness tokens around an exact numeric conversion that must still hit the cache.

pairs.a: How do I install numpy with pip?
pairs.b: how do i instal numpy wiht pip
pairs.note: Misspellings and lost capitalization must not stop the cache from hitting.

pairs.a: What is the difference between var and let in JavaScript?
pairs.b: whats teh diference betwen var and let in javascript
pairs.note: Several typos plus dropped punctuation over an unchanged question.

pairs.a: How long does it take to fly from London to New York?
pairs.b: how long dose it take to fly form london to new york
pairs.note: Transposed letters in common words with the same travel duration need.

pairs.a: What are the symptoms of dehydration?
pairs.b: wat r the symtoms of dehydration
pairs.note: Chat shorthand and misspelling of the key noun, identical information need.

pairs.a: How do I clear the npm cache?
pairs.b: how do i clera the npm cahce
pairs.note: Two transposition typos in a short prompt where each token carries a lot of weight.

pairs.a: How do I make cold brew coffee at home?
pairs.b: how do i mak cold brew coffe at home
pairs.note: Dropped trailing letters and no capitals, same recipe question.

pairs.a: python list reverse
pairs.b: How do I reverse a list in Python?
pairs.note: Keyword style search query versus a full natural language question.

pairs.a: git undo last commit
pairs.b: What is the command to undo the last commit I made in git?
pairs.note: Terse three word query expanded into a complete sentence with no new constraints.

pairs.a: docker remove all containers
pairs.b: How can I remove all of my Docker containers at once?
pairs.note: Padding words like at once do not narrow the request in any way.

pairs.a: capital of Peru
pairs.b: Can you tell me what the capital city of Peru is?
pairs.note: Fragment versus full question for the same single fact.

pairs.a: pandas drop column
pairs.b: How do I drop a column from a pandas DataFrame?
pairs.note: Library plus verb plus noun shorthand matching a fully spelled out question.

pairs.a: 375 f to c
pairs.b: What is 375 degrees Fahrenheit in Celsius?
pairs.note: Abbreviated unit conversion with the same numbers and the same direction.

pairs.a: css flexbox center vertically
pairs.b: How do I vertically center items using CSS flexbox?
pairs.note: Search style keywords versus prose, both wanting the same flexbox property.

pairs.a: In Python, how do I sort a dictionary by value?
pairs.b: How do I sort a dictionary by value in Python?
pairs.note: Leading versus trailing language qualifier with identical meaning.

pairs.a: What is the difference between a thread and a process?
pairs.b: What is the difference between a process and a thread?
pairs.note: Difference is symmetric here, unlike is A better than B, so this must still hit.

pairs.a: For a marathon, how many weeks of training do I need?
pairs.b: How many weeks of training do I need for a marathon?
pairs.note: Fronted prepositional phrase moved to the end with no semantic change.

pairs.a: Using Node, how do I read a file line by line?
pairs.b: How do I read a file line by line using Node?
pairs.note: Runtime qualifier repositioned, same file reading question.

pairs.a: What are the pros and cons of remote work?
pairs.b: What are the cons and pros of remote work?
pairs.note: Requesting both sides in either order asks for exactly the same content.

pairs.a: In Kotlin, what is the difference between val and var?
pairs.b: What is the difference between val and var in Kotlin?
pairs.note: Language qualifier moved across the sentence with identical intent.
